components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Gold background, near-black text. Used for primary actions like adding assets.

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Transparent background with border. Used for secondary actions.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: No border, no background until hover. Used for edit and cancel actions.

components.name: Card
components.description: Dashboard metric card with title and value. 1px border, no shadow.

components.name: Input
components.description: Text input with 1px border, 40px height, focus ring in gold.

components.description: Asset portfolio table with monospace numeric columns and P&L indicators.

narrative.overview: Gold Tracker is a personal portfolio journal — not a trading terminal, not a SaaS dashboard. It presents hard financial data with the calm authority of a well-kept ledger. Every screen should feel like a page from a serious collector's notebook: sparse, legible, and purposeful.
keyCharacteristics: Data-dense but visually sparse — more information through less decoration
Gold as a deliberate accent, not a theme
System fonts for speed and neutrality
Tonal layering over shadows for depth

rules.name: The Gold Rule
rules.body: Gold is the accent — never the surface. It appears on ≤15% of any given screen. Its rarity is what makes it meaningful.

rules.name: The No-Cream Rule
rules.body: The page background is a true neutral (near-zero chroma), not cream, sand, or parchment. Warmth lives in the accent, not the canvas.

rules.name: The Monospace-For-Numbers Rule
rules.body: All numeric data in tables uses a monospace font. This ensures decimal alignment and makes scanning P&L values effortless.

dos: Do use gold sparingly — as an accent on buttons, active states, and the logo.
Do let monospace fonts handle numeric alignment in tables.
Do use tonal layering (background vs. surface) for depth, not shadows.
Do keep the page background a true neutral — no cream, no beige, no parchment.
Do use system fonts for instant rendering and zero font-loading jank.
Do pair P&L color indicators with +/− symbols for color-blind accessibility.
donts: Don't use gradient backgrounds on cards, buttons, or text.
Don't use cream, sand, beige, parchment, or any warm near-white as a page background.
Don't use identical card grids — cards are for dashboard metrics only.
Don't use box-shadows on cards or containers (except focus rings).
Don't use color alone to convey P&L — always include a +/− symbol.
Don't use more than one accent color per screen (gold OR green, not both).
Don't use pill-shaped buttons (100% border-radius) except for tags and chips.
Don't use tracked uppercase eyebrow text above section headings.
